publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 30, 2020 at 04:09

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James Cummins Bookseller on the Jonathan O. Bulkley Fund, 2020.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Writings, 1886-1925. II. Ellen Horne Kemp Illustrations and Notes, 1889-1970. III. Other Papers, 1897-1915.

bioghist.head: Frederic M. (Frederic Michael) Halford (1844-1914)
bioghist.p: Frederic M. (Frederic Michael) Halford (1844-1914), British angler and author of books on dry-fly fishing.

bioghist.head: Ellen Horne Kemp (1846-)
bioghist.p: Ellen Horne Kemp (1846-), British artist and illustrator of books by Frederic M. Halford. Ellen Horne Kemp married Robert Kemp (1832-) in 1869; their daughter Jane Horne Kemp (1870-) married Bryce Leicester in 1903; and their son Robert Kemp, (1871-) was a malacologist.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Frederic M. Halford Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of books by Halford on dry-fly fishing and creation of artificial flies, and a draft of an autobiography, 1886-1912; scrapbooks containing clippings of reviews of books by Halford, 1897-1911, and clippings of obituaries and other articles about Halford, 1914-1915; and papers of Ellen Horne Kemp, 1896-1907, consisting of proofs of illustrations for books by Halford and related notes. Most drafts by Halford are autograph manuscript or typescript, with revisions and printer's annotations, some incorporating writings or annotations by J. J. Hardy, W. A. Hunter, George Selwyn Marryat, William Senior, or G. E. M. Skues. Some drafts are accompanied by galley or page proofs with revisions, photographs and printed proofs for plates; or contracts and accounts with publishers George Routledge and Sons or Vinton & Company. Kemp's papers contain hand-colored and uncolored proofs of her drawings of artificial flies, with related autograph manuscript notes; notes and proofs for illustrations by Duncan Moul; and a few drawings by Kemp and letters to her from Halford and Vinton & Company. Kemp's papers are accompanied by autograph manuscript notes, circa 1970, by Phoebe Leicester, a former owner and possibly a descendant of Kemp's daughter Jane Horne Kemp Leicester.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Frederic M. Halford Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

persname.part: Skues, G. E. M. (George Edward Mackenzie), 1958-1979

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

did.unittitle: An angler's autobiography. Halford dry-fly series, volume 4 . (London: Vinton & Company, 1903)
Draft, autograph manuscript with revisions; with annotations by William Senior
Part I. Draft, autograph manuscript with revisions and printer's annotations
Title pages for first and deluxe editions, and table of contents
Part I. Typescript mimeograph with revisions
Title pages for first and deluxe editions, table of contents, and list of plates
Part II. Galley proof with revisions and printer's annotations
Part III. Draft, autograph manuscript and printed article fragments from The Field with printer's annotations; with writings by G. E. M. Skues and W. F. Brougham
The dry-fly man's handbook (London: George Routledge and Sons, 1913)
Contract for publication by George Routledge and Sons. Drafts, typescript and typescript carbon
Part I, chapter 1. Notes on fishing rods by J. J. Hardy, of Hardy Brothers Ltd., and W. A. Hunter, of C. Farlow & Co., typescript; with annotations by Halford
Plates. Photographs and printed proofs, with notes
Draft, typescript carbon with revisions and printers' annotations
Part I, chapter 2-Part III. Galley proof with revisions (incomplete)
Part I, chapter 1. Page proof with revisions
Title page, preliminary pages, table of contents, list of plates, and preface
Floating flies and how to dress them (London: Sampson Low, Marston, Searle, & Rivington, 1886)
Autograph manuscript draft with revisions and printers' annotations; with annotations by George Selwyn Marryat
Modern development of the dry fly (London: George Routledge and Sons, 1910)
Contract for publication by George Routledge and Sons. Drafts, typescript and typescript carbon
Draft, bound by George Routledge and Sons, autograph manuscript with revisions and printer's annotations; with drawings and printed plates
Ellen Horne Kemp Illustrations and Notes

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

did.unittitle: Plate proofs, hand colored, with notes
Dry-fly fishing in theory and practice (London: Sampson Low, Marston, Searle, & Rivington, 1889)
Illustrations by Duncan Moul. Plate proofs, color lithograph, with notes
Reviews of books by Halford. Printed clippings in a scrapbook
"In memoriam F. M. H. 1914." Printed clippings of obituaries and other articles about Halford in a scrapbook
